description: Dual case: Donald Norby stole confidential documents from SwiftLogic Inc. and was found dead. He was negotiating the sale of Palomino product documents with Mr. E. Yob Taog's phone was compromised with malware that exfiltrated PDFs.
tier_note: Evidencia digital no verificable en este momento. No usar hasta obtener hashes canónicos.

artifacts.content: SMS/Malware C&C: Norby arranged installation of malware on Taog's phone | Negotiations with Mr. E about document pricing | Malware messages confirming file exfiltration | Email/SMS: Norby requesting higher payment and sending samples
forensic_anomalies: SMS from malware confirming periodic exfiltration of PDFs
Negotiation of intellectual property sale via SMS/email

artifacts.content: HTML Cache: Page with access to remote server IP 50.56.29.109 | Malware code: Hardcoded references to exfiltration server | Download logs: Systematic downloads of confidential PDF documents
forensic_anomalies: Hardcoded reference to external server 50.56.29.109 in malware code
Systematic downloads of confidential PDFs at regular intervals

artifacts.content: Confidential SwiftLogic PDF files on SD card | Product: Palomino | Photo EXIF metadata: Motorola Droid | Browser history: Searches on SwiftLogic employees (prior reconnaissance)
forensic_anomalies: Confidential competitor documents on phone SD card
Search history on target company employees indicates prior reconnaissance

artifacts.content: Contact Records: Links between Norby, Mr. E, and Yob Taog | Negotiations: Initial price offered, Norby asked for more, sent sample | Death: Donald Norby found shot, potential connection to criminal group KRYPTIX
forensic_anomalies: Industrial espionage negotiation pattern with renegotiation attempt
Suspect's death under violent circumstances after attempting to raise the price
